mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession risk, triggering downward earnings revisions and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with elevated consumer price inflation compress real household disposable income and signal macroeconomic overheating, forcing mean-reverting equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or partial correlation with forward returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during supply chain normalization directly expands corporate operating margins, driving positive short-term equity outperformance as earnings beat expectations.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO acceleration during supply shocks, or the signal yields negative median returns across three consecutive rolling quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, leading to immediate downward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Retail sales contractions do not precede negative 20-day forward returns in walk-forward tests, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 45% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth reflects broad economic expansion and rising aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive quarterly equity returns as earnings momentum builds.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across multiple decades, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high policy rates and sticky inflation compress real yields and equity risk premiums, forcing multiple contraction and negative long-term forward returns as valuation multiples reset.
failure_condition: High rate and inflation combinations fail to predict negative 60-day returns in historical backtests, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1.5% annualized GDP growth signals economic stagnation, triggering widespread earnings downgrades and negative quarterly equity performance as corporate profit margins contract.
failure_condition: GDP growth below threshold does not correspond to negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or the forward return distribution shows no left-skew during triggered periods.
